metadata.summary: Implement tool/function calling and structured JSON output with Ollama. Requires Llama 3.1+ with 32k+ context. Covers tool schema definition, execution loop, and Pydantic structured outputs.

content.title: Ollama Tool Calling and Structured Outputs
content.summary: Ollama supports OpenAI-compatible tool/function calling and JSON schema structured outputs. Tool calling requires Llama 3.1 8B or higher with num_ctx set to at least 32768. Structured output uses the format parameter with a Pydantic-derived JSON schema.

why.p: Tool calling enables local LLMs to invoke external functions (database queries, API calls, calculations) based on conversation context — the same capability that makes cloud LLMs useful for agents. Structured outputs constrain the model to produce valid JSON matching a known schema, eliminating post-processing and parse errors in pipelines. Both features are critical for replacing cloud LLM calls with local alternatives in agent architectures.

list.item: Building a local agent that needs to call external tools or APIs without cloud API costs.
Extracting structured data (entities, classifications, analysis) from text with schema validation.
Replacing cloud LLM tool-calling in CI pipelines or privacy-sensitive workflows.
Testing agent tool-calling logic locally before deploying against cloud models.
Models smaller than 13B — tool calling reliability degrades significantly; Llama 3.1 8B is the practical minimum.
Context window below 32k — the model returns text instead of JSON tool calls silently.
Complex multi-tool chains where frontier cloud models (GPT-4o, Claude) produce significantly more reliable results.
Structured output schemas with deeply nested objects — compliance varies by model; test thoroughly before production.

content.warning: Tool calling fails silently if context window is too small: the model returns plain text instead of JSON tool calls. Always set num_ctx to at least 32768 for tool use. Validate that tool_calls is present in the response before processing.

example.title: Tool calling with execution loop
example.note: Always set num_ctx to 32768 in options; tool calls fail silently at smaller context sizes.

example.title: Tool calling without context window override
example.code: # Missing num_ctx — tool calls will fail silently on most models response = ollama.chat( model="llama3.1:8b", messages=messages, tools=tools # No options={"num_ctx": 32768} ) # model returns plain text instead of tool_calls JSON
example.note: Without num_ctx override, Ollama uses the Modelfile default (often 2048 or 4096), which is insufficient for tool schemas.

section.name: structured-outputs
section.p: Structured outputs use the format parameter with a JSON Schema (or Pydantic model_json_schema()). The model is constrained to produce JSON matching that schema. Compliance varies by model — test your specific model+schema combination before production.

example.title: Structured output with Pydantic schema
example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel import ollama import json
class ProductReview(BaseModel): sentiment: str # positive, negative, neutral score: float # 0.0 to 1.0 summary: str pros: list[str] cons: list[str]
def analyze_review(review_text: str, model: str = "llama3.1:8b") -> ProductReview: """Analyze a review and return structured data.""" response = ollama.chat( model=model, messages=[ { "role": "system", "content": "You are a review analyzer. Return your analysis as JSON." }, { "role": "user", "content": f"Analyze this review:\n\n{review_text}" } ], format=ProductReview.model_json_schema() )
data = json.loads(response["message"]["content"]) return ProductReview(**data)
review = """ Great laptop! Fast performance, beautiful display, and excellent battery life. The keyboard could be better though, and it runs a bit hot under load. Overall, very satisfied with my purchase. """
analysis = analyze_review(review) print(f"Sentiment: {analysis.sentiment}") print(f"Score: {analysis.score}") print(f"Pros: {analysis.pros}") print(f"Cons: {analysis.cons}")

example.title: Relying on prompt-only JSON without format parameter
example.code: # Fragile: model may return markdown code fences, trailing text, or invalid JSON response = ollama.chat( model="llama3.1:8b", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Return JSON: {sentiment, score}"}] # Missing: format=schema ) # Frequently returns ```json\n{...}\n``` which json.loads() rejects
example.note: Without the format parameter, models frequently wrap JSON in markdown code fences or add explanatory text, causing parse failures.

rule.statement: Always set options={"num_ctx": 32768} when using tool calling — the default context size causes silent failures where the model returns text instead of JSON tool calls.
rule.rationale: Tool schemas consume hundreds to thousands of tokens; models truncate context silently and fall back to plain text output without warning.

rule.statement: Use models Llama 3.1 8B or larger for tool calling; validate that response["message"].get("tool_calls") is truthy before processing tool results.
rule.rationale: Smaller models produce inconsistent tool call JSON; checking presence before iteration prevents KeyError and silent data loss.

rule.statement: Pass Pydantic model_json_schema() as the format parameter for structured outputs rather than relying on system prompt instructions alone.
rule.rationale: The format parameter enables constrained decoding; prompt-only instructions are unreliable and produce unparseable output ~20% of the time.

rule.statement: Test structured output compliance for your exact model+schema combination before committing to it in production — compliance rates vary across models and schema complexity.
rule.rationale: A schema that works reliably with llama3.1:8b may fail 10-30% of the time with a different model version or quantization.

checklist.check: Defined tool schemas with complete JSON Schema (type, properties, required)
Set num_ctx to 32768 or higher in options
Implemented tool execution handlers for all declared tools
Added tool result formatting (role: "tool", content: json.dumps(result))
Tested with prompts that should and should not trigger tools
For structured outputs: validated compliance across 20+ samples
